summary: Completed comprehensive testing of recursive horse parsing functionality in V85 Travanalys app. The recursive parser successfully finds horse objects anywhere in JSON structures and maps fields correctly.

ui_bugs.issue: Race name and track name not correctly extracted from nested structures
ui_bugs.details: When race info is nested (e.g., competition.race.name), fallback values are used instead of extracting the actual values

ui_bugs.issue: Missing React key props causing console warnings
ui_bugs.details: Console warnings about missing unique key props in list rendering

data_handling_issues.issue: Horses with missing odds/betDistribution show NaN values instead of being filtered
data_handling_issues.details: The validation logic should filter out horses with missing required data, but they appear in table with NaN values

passed_tests: ATG format (nested starts array) parsing successful
Horse object identification (postPosition, horse.name, pools) works
All analysis calculations (value ratio, ranking score, play recommendations) work
Error handling for 'no horses found' works correctly
Recursive search finds horses at any depth in JSON structure
Console warnings shown for skipped horses (partial functionality)
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix race metadata extraction from nested structures - the parser should correctly extract race.name and track.name from nested objects instead of falling back to default values. Also fix horse validation to properly filter out horses with missing odds/betDistribution instead of showing NaN values.
